text.text:
I've come here twice within the week and what a great addition to SOMA especially for the in-office folks.

I've tried the seasonal cake slice, burnt sugar cake slice, seasonal danish, passionfruit kouign amann, orange blossom cardamom bun and a chicken sausage breakfast sandwich.

The seasonal Danish was everything seasoning crusted croissant filled with chives and dill cream cheese. This was my favorite pastry by far. Perfect ratio and flakiness. Both times it didn't stand a chance of living more than a minute.

The seasonal cake slice was strawberry matcha which is trendy but I preferred the burnt sugar cake way more. Not too sweet. Lovely crumble all around and the icing pulled it together so well.

Passion fruit kouign amann was tasty but I felt there was barely any curd so after a few bites it was just ok. Same with the orange blossom cardamom bun. Not a huge fan but worth a shot and everything else was fire.

The breakfast sandwich was probably one of the best I've had in the city. I'll admit i was skeptical but they know how to bake here, very well. I ate it inside and they warmed it up and put it on a nice plate. The egg looked like McDonald's brekkie sandos but it had a great texture that held with the melted cheese and savory chicken sausage with the warm and toasty buns to round it out. Order this and enjoy it here with a pastry! For 6 items with tax and tip came out to $50 which isn't bad.

They are in a soft launch as of now. Definitely worth checking out and potentially could be a regular go-to for those in close proximity.

The interior is spotless and so spacious it was nice to relax and eat with ease. The staff is super friendly and attentive. For

text.text: came here with high expectations since we heard how much a lot of ppl love this place. We ordered the everything croissant but was disappointed about how little filling there was of the chive cream cheese (literally a teaspoon size). don’t know if it’s related to them not having a grand opening yet but felt like it lacked the butteriness and flakiness of a croissant that we were hoping for. They have a good amount of seating though and staff were all very friendly so hopefully the grand opening helps improve things!

They have a good amount of seating though and staff were all very friendly so hopefully the grand opening helps improve things!

text.text:
Got a bunch of pastries to share with coworkers. Reliable almond croissant, delightful passionfruit kouign-amann. Had a mochi muffin for the first time and it was pretty great too! Very flaky and light pain au chocolat.
Quick and friendly service, the chocolate-pistachio croissant was already sold out at 9:30am on a random weekday!

originalText.text:

text.text:
Meh. $52.52 not worth. Rather go for an actual breakfast.
Atmosphere:
Fantastic, super cozy and feels inviting. Soft music. Only downfall is no pricing on the pastries 👀
Service:
The people are really nice and attentive. Said hellos, explained what they had, the recommendations. Overall smooth.
Food:
Apple Juice was a thick, real nice texture
Croissant was like the crust of a sourdough loaf, no layers, all shell and hollow. Check out the pictures.
Passion fruit Kouign-amann was the complete star of the show! 10/10 super soft flaky layered buttery, everything the croissant wants to be.
Breakfast sandwiches at $15 each is insane for what was given. Egg was a mushy microwaves puck, just overall not worth.

text.text: I visited in the morning and was greeted by very friendly staff! I got a few pastries, a chocolate chip cookie and a slice of cake. The pistachio croissant was SO delicious!! The chocolate chip cookie was soft and had the perfect amount of salt on it. I was so excited to try everything, I only got a picture of the cake😆 Looking forward to trying more!
